description: Regression: given a bare domain + an action verb ('apply for mahdif.com at notion'), the agent READS the link first (fetch/scrape/search) and lets what it reads decide the specifics, instead of throwing a clarifying card. Pins the 'a link the user drops is an instruction to LOOK' rule in <interpreting_intent>. The portfolio already answers 'which role' (it's a designer's), so a generic department/role menu is a failure.

judge.rubric: The user dropped a bare domain plus an action verb: 'apply for mahdif.com at notion'. PASS if the assistant treats this as a job application to Notion AND investigates mahdif.com (fetch/scrape it, or web-search the name) before — or instead of — asking anything, then proceeds toward the application (finding/searching Notion roles, drafting/submitting the application). Reading mahdif.com and inferring the person's field (e.g. that they're a designer, so design roles) is exactly the right move. FAIL if the assistant instead (a) hands back a menu of unrelated interpretations (register the domain, become a Notion partner, set up a workspace), (b) asks the user to pick which Notion role/department to apply for when the portfolio it could read would answer that, or (c) raises any clarifying question card before looking at mahdif.com. Investigating-then-acting = PASS; asking-before-looking or offering an interpretation/department menu = FAIL.
